faqs.question: Why are drain flies common in Huguenot homes?
faqs.answer: Huguenot's proximity to water bodies like Lemon Creek and the Great Kills area can contribute to higher humidity levels, while many older homes in the neighborhood have aging plumbing that creates breeding sites for drain flies. These small flies thrive in the organic matter that builds up in drains, sewer lines, and damp areas common in both vintage and modern homes.

faqs.question: What does your drain fly treatment include?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive treatment includes a thorough inspection of all drains, pipes, and potential breeding sites, mechanical cleaning of affected drains, application of bio-enzymatic treatments to eliminate organic buildup, and targeted applications of safe insecticides when necessary. We also provide recommendations for preventing future infestations specific to your property.

faqs.answer: We typically offer same-day or next-day service to Huguenot residents. Emergency services are also available for severe infestations.

faqs.question: Is the treatment safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We prioritize the safety of your household by using EPA-approved products and eco-friendly bio-enzymatic cleaners that are safe for children and pets. Our technicians are fully trained in safe application methods and will provide you with any necessary precautions specific to your treatment plan.
